concerns.text: FR-007 and SC-001 require comparing high-res semantic similarity against a 'low-res baseline' and calculating the percentage difference. Tasks T026-T029 only implement high-res extraction and threshold flagging. There is no task to generate the low-res baseline scores (e.g., running eval on 64x64 images) or perform the comparative subtraction required by the spec.

FR-002 (6-hour CPU training limit) and SC-003 (runtime measurement) are untestable. Task T016 (logging) is marked as unchecked `[ ]`, and T014 (checkpoint saving) is unchecked. Without logging, the runtime cannot be measured; without a checkpoint, the training loop cannot be verified to converge within the timebox.

SC-005 mandates a 'one-sample t-test' against the low-resolution baseline mean. Task T022 implements a 'paired t-test/Wilcoxon' per the plan's amendment. While the plan notes this deviation, the task list does not include a corresponding task to update the Success Criteria definition or formally document the amendment in the spec traceability, leaving a gap between the documented success definition and the implemented verification method.

Task T008 includes a comment `<!-- SKIPPED: non-mapping output -->` which suggests the test implementation was skipped or incomplete. This contradicts the requirement for foundational tests to exist and fail initially to drive development.
